summary: Ukrainian forces intensified UAV strikes across multiple fronts, targeting Russian logistics hubs including the Grushova oil terminal in Novorossiysk and striking deep into occupied territories, while Russian air defenses shot down over 100 hostile drones on 23 May. Heavy fighting continued on the Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia fronts with Ukrainian assault aircraft advancing up to 1.2 km in several sectors, and Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia. Civilian casualties rose from drone attacks in Marhanets (13 injured) and a college dormitory in the LNR (10 dead, 38 injured).
key_points: Ukrainian UAVs struck the Grushova oil terminal in Novorossiysk, a major Russian fuel hub, causing a fire (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).
Russian air defenses downed 102 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and Parody types, after a massive 124‑drone attack from multiple directions (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian assault aircraft of the "North" Group advanced up to 1.2 km on three sectors of the Kharkiv front, notably near Budarki and Kuibyshevsky district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, indicating escalation of air‑to‑ground strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62486).
Ukrainian UAV groups reported in Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv and Chernihiv regions, moving toward key logistics nodes such as Zavodske, Kalijivka and Kupchichi (Source: @eRadarrua/93114).
Russian FPV drone attack on Marhanets injured 13 civilians, shattering vehicle windows (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).
LNR college dormitory attack resulted in 10 dead and 38 injured, highlighting civilian toll of cross‑border strikes (Source: @tass_agency/377178).
Ukrainian forces recaptured Verkhnya Tersa in Zaporozhzhia, a strategic logistical node, after intense combined‑arms operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAVs struck the Grushova oil terminal in Novorossiysk, inflicting a fire on a key Russian fuel hub (Source: @eRadarrua/93103). Russian air defenses shot down 102 hostile drones after a massive 124‑drone raid (Source: @kpszsu/62474). Ukrainian assault aircraft advanced up to 1.2 km on multiple Kharkiv sectors, while Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377; @kpszsu/62486).

points: Ukrainian UAVs attacked the Grushova oil terminal in Novorossiysk, igniting a fire at one of Russia's largest oil storage complexes (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).

Assault aircraft of the "North" Group of Forces advanced up to 800 m on twenty sectors of the Sumy front and up to 1.2 km on three Kharkiv sectors, including Budarki and Kuibyshevsky district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).

Ukrainian strike UAV groups were positioned in Poltava (heading to Zavodske, Chornukhy), Sumy (heading to Kalijivka, Pershe Travnya), Kharkiv (heading to Babai) and Chernihiv (heading to Kupchichi, Polova) indicating deep‑strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/93114).

Ukrainian forces recaptured Verkhnya Tersa in Zaporozhzhia, a critical logistical and defensive node, after coordinated action by the 155th Marine Infantry Regiment and motor rifle units (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Chernihiv region heading toward Nosivka and over Kyiv region heading south, suggesting pressure on northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62468; @kpszsu/62483).

Ukrainian drone activity was noted over Odesa region (2 near Mayaki from Vypasnoe, 1 Vylkove) and Black Sea heading toward Tatarbunary, indicating southern maritime threats (Source: @eRadarrua/93105; @kpszsu/62477).

Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Kyiv districts (Shahed over Golosiiv, Nyvky, Svyatoshyn, and near Bucha), highlighting continued air‑defence challenges in the capital region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66913; @mon1tor_ua/66901; @mon1tor_ua/66900).
Ukrainian forces reported high‑intelligence UAV activity over Nikopol and strike UAVs over Kherson, suggesting reconnaissance and strike coordination in the south (Source: @eRadarrua/93113).

Russian air defence shot down 102 enemy UAVs on the night of 23 May after a coordinated attack involving 124 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and Parody drones from directions including Kursk, Shatalovo and Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62486).
A fire broke out at an oil depot in Novorossiysk during Ukrainian attacks, indicating damage to Russian fuel infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian forces reported a civilian killed in a moving vehicle in Starodub district, Bryansk, after a Ukrainian drone strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa (Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi district), posing a threat to Russian‑controlled southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/62479).
Russian forces claimed liberation of Verkhnya Tersa in Zaporozhzhia, though Ukrainian reports indicate ongoing contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30359).
Russian airspace restrictions were imposed at several airports following increased UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian defense ministry reported damage to multi‑story building in Kursk north‑west microdistrict from Ukrainian attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian assault aircraft advanced up to 800 m on twenty sectors of the Sumy front, with skirmishes reported in Zapselye and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian forces reported progress up to 1.2 km in three sectors of the Kharkiv front, including Budarki and forested Kuibyshevsky district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAV groups operating from Sumy region moved toward Kalijivka and Pershe Travnya, indicating pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @eRadarrua/93114).
Russian UAV attacks hit civilian targets in Belgorod region (Shebekino cargo vehicle, Yasnye Zori enterprise), causing injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near Chernihiv heading toward Kupchichi, threatening eastern approaches (Source: @eRadarrua/93114).
Ukrainian strike UAVs were observed over Kharkiv heading to Babai, suggesting targeting of logistics nodes (Source: @eRadarrua/93114).
Russian air defence downed multiple UAVs over Kaluga districts (Maloyaroslavets, Borovsk, Tarus) indicating defensive actions on the eastern flank (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian forces reported heavy battles near Konstantinovka and flanks, with no significant territorial change (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAVs detected over Chernihiv region heading toward Nosivka and Kyiv region, indicating pressure on northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62468; @kpszsu/62483).
Ukrainian assault aircraft advanced up to 800 m on twenty sectors of the Sumy front, with skirmishes in Zapselye and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian air defence shot down 102 enemy UAVs across northern sectors during the large‑scale drone raid (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near Kyiv districts (Shahed over Golosiiv, Nyvky, Svyatoshyn, and near Bucha), testing capital air defences (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66913; @mon1tor_ua/66901; @mon1tor_ua/66900).
Ukrainian UAV activity noted near Vytut‑Lytovska in Golosiiv district, indicating continued incursions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66913).
Ukrainian UAV loss reported low altitude near unspecified location, suggesting operational attrition (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66902).
Russian civilian casualty in Bryansk (Starodub district) from Ukrainian drone strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAVs observed over Kyiv (Kozhanka, Fastiv) and Khmelnytsky (Hrytsiv) indicating reconnaissance activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93101).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked the Grushova oil terminal in Novorossiysk, a key southern Russian fuel hub (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62486).
Ukrainian forces recaptured Verkhnya Tersa in Zaporozhzhia, a strategic logistical node (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported over Dnipropetrovsk (Nikopol) and Kherson, indicating southern strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/93113).
Russian FPV drone attack on Marhanets injured 13 civilians, highlighting southern civilian risk (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).
Ukrainian UAVs observed from Black Sea heading toward Odesa (Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi district), threatening southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/62479).
Ukrainian assault aircraft advanced up to 1.2 km on three sectors of the Kharkiv front, affecting southern logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia under constant Ukrainian drone attack, causing power disruptions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian air defence downed 102 hostile UAVs after a massive 124‑drone raid involving Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and Parody types (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian assault aircraft of the "North" Group advanced up to 800 m on Sumy front and up to 1.2 km on Kharkiv front, indicating increased air‑ground coordination (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, escalating aerial strike intensity (Source: @kpszsu/62486).
Ukrainian UAV groups positioned in multiple oblasts (Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv) with directed strikes toward logistical nodes (Source: @eRadarrua/93114).
Ukrainian UAVs observed over the Black Sea heading south toward Odesa and Tatarbunary, expanding the aerial threat envelope (Source: @kpszsu/62477; @kpszsu/62479).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused a fire at an oil depot in Novorossiysk, demonstrating reach into Russian interior airspace (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian airspace restrictions imposed at several airports due to heightened UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAV loss at low altitude reported, indicating attrition rates for both sides (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66902).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs struck the Grushova oil terminal in Novorossiysk, igniting a fire at a major Russian fuel hub (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).
Russian air defence downed 102 enemy UAVs after a coordinated 124‑drone attack from multiple directions (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian assault aircraft advanced up to 1.2 km on three Kharkiv sectors, increasing pressure on Russian defensive lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, marking a significant escalation in aerial strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62486).
Ukrainian forces recaptured Verkhnya Tersa in Zaporozhzhia, a key logistical node, after combined‑arms operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
FPV drone attack on Marhanets injured 13 civilians, highlighting the threat to southern civilian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).
LNR college dormitory attack resulted in 10 deaths and 38 injuries, underscoring civilian casualties from cross‑border strikes (Source: @tass_agency/377178).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are expanding their UAV strike envelope, reaching deep into Russian territory and targeting critical energy infrastructure, while Russian air defences are increasingly effective, downing over a hundred hostile drones in a single night. Russian tactical aviation's use of guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia signals a shift toward higher‑precision, high‑impact strikes, raising the risk to Ukrainian front‑line and rear positions. Both sides are sustaining civilian casualties, with drone attacks on populated areas in the LNR and Marhanets, indicating a growing threat to non‑combatants.
sections.regional_response: Western allies continue to provide air‑defence support, as evidenced by the high UAV interception rates, while Russia imposes airport restrictions to manage air‑space congestion. Ukraine's diplomatic outreach, including negotiations for civilian evacuations around Olenek, reflects a focus on mitigating humanitarian fallout amid intensified combat. Russian statements about liberating Verkhnya Tersa contrast with Ukrainian claims, highlighting contested narratives in the Zaporizhzhia region.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of targeting logistics corridors in the north and south, suggesting Ukrainian intent to disrupt supply lines. The concentration of Russian guided bomb deployments in Zaporizhzhia may indicate preparation for a larger offensive or defensive counter‑measure. Continued civilian casualties from drone strikes necessitate enhanced air‑defence coverage around urban centers and critical infrastructure.
